title: Tools
subtitle: Extend the AI agent with custom tools: REST calls, SQL queries or sub-prompts. Every enabled tool is offered to the agent in your conversations.

empty.title: No custom tools
empty.subtitle: Create your first tool to extend the agent's capabilities

prompt.userHint: — optional. If empty: parameters are serialized as JSON
prompt.help: How it works: when the main agent calls this tool, the parameters are interpolated into the templates and a separate LLM sub-call runs with the selected model. The response is returned as the tool result. Select "Use the default" to use the LLM set as default in settings.

params.label: Parameters ({{count}})
params.intro: Parameters become the fields the LLM fills before invoking the tool. The description is critical: the more precise it is, the better the model understands what to enter.
params.empty: No parameters — the tool is invoked without input.
params.name: Name
params.descPlaceholder: Search terms to use as the query

secrets.label: Secrets ({{count}})
secrets.introPre: API keys and tokens are encrypted with AES-256 before saving. Use
secrets.introPost: to reference them in the config.

test.label: Test (dry-run)
test.needSave: Save the tool to enable testing.
test.argsHint: — values the LLM would pass to the tool

rag.modeSearchNote: The tool searches for relevant documents based on the LLM's query.
rag.modeIndexNote: The tool indexes a file or text into the collection, making it searchable.
rag.noCollections: No collection found in Qdrant. Make sure the Vector DB is connected.
rag.scopeNote: Leave it on Automatic: the filter adapts to the chat context on its own. Change it only for a dedicated tool (e.g. company base only or cross-project search).
rag.searchHelp: How it works: the tool runs a semantic search in the chosen collection. The LLM provides the query (text to search); the tool returns the most relevant chunks with source and score. Add the parameter query (string, required) and optionally limit (number, optional) in the Parameters section.
rag.indexScopeAuto: Automatic (project if the chat has one, otherwise personal)
rag.fileIdDesc: Name of the parameter the LLM will use to pass the fileId. The backend uses pdf-parse/OCR to extract the text.
rag.textParamDesc: Name of the parameter that will contain the raw text to index. Alternative to fileId.
rag.metadataDesc: Names of additional parameters that will be saved as metadata on the vector chunks.
rag.indexHelp: How it works: the tool indexes into the chosen collection. If you configure a fileId parameter, the LLM must pass a fileId (shown in messages as id: xxx); the backend extracts the text natively. If you configure a text parameter, the LLM must provide the text directly. At least one of the two is required.

sql.noDataSourcePre: No data source configured. Go to
sql.modeFreeDesc: The LLM generates the full SELECT
sql.templateNote: Only SELECT allowed · LIMIT added automatically if missing
sql.queryParamHint: — the LLM fills this param with the SELECT
sql.queryParamWarn: Add a parameter "{{param}}" of type string, not required, in the Parameters section. The LLM omits it on the first call to fetch the schema, then fills it with the SELECT.
sql.schemaHintsNotePre: Schema (tables, fields, relations) and notes are configured in the
sql.schemaHintsNotePost: — shared across all tools using the same DB.
sql.schemaModeCompactDesc: Relations + table names + comments, no columns (lightweight). The model discovers columns with an exploratory query.
sql.schemaModeFullDesc: Each table with columns, annotated FKs and localized relations. Self-contained but more tokens.
sql.opsNotePost: is always on. Enable writes only if needed: the DB account should have the matching minimal privileges.

identity.descHint: — the LLM reads this to decide when to use the tool
identity.descPlaceholder: E.g.: "Search the web via Brave Search. Use it when the user asks for fresh news, recent regulations or market prices. Do NOT use for internal company data."
identity.loadOnFirstTitle: If disabled, the tool is not loaded into the chat: it stays usable only by an agent that includes it among its tools (hierarchical delegation).

mongo.noDataSource: No MongoDB data source. Create one in Data Sources with the MongoDB engine.
mongo.modeTemplateDesc: Fixed collection and operation, filter/pipeline with :param
mongo.modeFreeDesc: The model writes the operation spec as JSON
mongo.queryParamHint: — the model writes the JSON spec { collection, op, filter|pipeline } here

redis.noDataSource: No Redis data source. Create one in Data Sources with the Redis engine.
redis.queryParamHint: — the model writes { command, args } here
redis.allowWriteHint: (read-only by default; admin commands always blocked)
